settings.title: How to Water a New Shrub Without Drowning It
settings.excerpt: Most shrubs we replace did not die of thirst, they died of daily light sprinkles that never reached the roots.
settings.body:
The question we hear most after a planting job is how often to water. The honest answer surprises people: less often than you think, but much more deeply.

A daily splash wets the top inch and evaporates by noon. The roots stay dry and grow shallow, which makes the plant weaker the following summer.

Water slow and deep. Let a hose trickle at the base for ten minutes, twice a week, in the first season. Then check the soil with your finger before the next watering.

Mulch a hand's depth around the base, but keep it off the stem. That single habit holds moisture and cuts your watering in half.
